response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant high-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a strong match for the AI-native environment. TypeScript is well beyond the required working level. However, Python — a must-have — appears nowhere on the resume, the deep systems work was largely in DLang rather than the required stack, and evidence of staff-level technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership is thin, with the Staff title held for only six months. Worth a conversation if Python can be verified, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency async I/O)
Recent AI-native experience: MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq
Strong TypeScript/Node depth with broad full-stack range across web, mobile, and embedded
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have skill
Limited demonstrated staff-level technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder management
Core high-performance experience is in DLang, a niche language with uncertain transferability to the required stack
